evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` combines normalization/logging with a browser event transport and singleton notifier lifecycle (`configureErrorNotifier`, `window.addEventListener`, module-level mutable state), making one highly-imported module own multiple architectural responsibilities.

`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` exports both low-level design-system wrappers and a stateful touch interaction implementation (`TouchableTooltip`) that installs document-level listeners, while multiple features consume the same shared entrypoint (`CreativePartnerPane.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`, `InfoTooltip.tsx`).

Gallery behavior is spread across orchestration-heavy hooks with large nested context bags (`useMediaGalleryController.ts`, `useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`) plus UI action components with large grouped prop interfaces (`ShotActions.tsx`), which obscures ownership boundaries.

Install-instructions flow is split across a key decision tree (`scenarios.ts`) and a separate manual key union/registry (`visuals.tsx`) consumed by `InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`, creating cross-file synchronization burden for one feature path.
`useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` centralizes query prefetching, image queue control, and cache eviction in one hook, creating a broad coupling point between data-fetch and UI preloading concerns.

Edge helper seams use incompatible failure contracts: `EdgeOperationResult` in `_shared/edgeOperation.ts` and `billing.ts`, but `complete_task/storage.ts` throws for upload failure (`handleStorageOperations`) while returning typed `thumbnailOutcome` for thumbnail failures.

Invalidation orchestration exposes multiple scheduling semantics in one module: `useInvalidateGenerations` dedupes delayed calls per shot with `timeoutRefs`, while `scheduleGenerationsInvalidation` does raw `setTimeout` with no dedupe/cancel path.

Multiple frontend integration modules capture Supabase client at import time (`generationTaskBridge.ts`, `useLineageChain.ts`, `generationTransformers.ts`, `generateVideoService.ts`), while edge storage helpers receive clients as parameters (`complete_task/storage.ts`), producing inconsistent boundary injection patterns.

Shot-selection contract is split across incompatible seams: typed event `selectShotForAddition` carries `{ shotId, shotName }`, context API only accepts `shotId`, and editor props keep `selectedShotId` and `shotName` separately.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts keeps both BuildTravelRequestBodyLegacyParams and BuildTravelRequestBodyV2Params plus adapter exports (_buildTravelRequestBodyLegacy), which duplicates local parameter plumbing instead of a single internal flow.

buildTimelinePairConfig in generateVideoService builds frame gaps from a sorted list but reads per-pair overrides from unsorted filteredShotGenerations by index, creating implicit ordering assumptions in local control flow.

Aspect-ratio helper logic is duplicated across src/shared/lib/imageAspectRatio.ts and src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts with separate fallback behavior, increasing drift risk in simple utility internals.

src/shared/lib/taskTypeUtils.ts uses taskType.includes('travel') in isTravelTaskType while task config/visibility lookups in src/shared/lib/taskConfig.ts rely on exact task-type keys, creating imprecise and inconsistent low-level classification behavior.

supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts has dead/legacy-style function parameters in generateThumbnail (_sourceBytes, _userId, _taskId) and recomputes objectPath despite already receiving it, indicating migration residue in function internals.
src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx implements overlapping device heuristics across useIsMobile/useIsTablet/useIsTouchDevice/useDeviceInfo, each with separate state/effects.
src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx composes multiple mobile/tablet signals plus an extra window-width fallback, showing the abstraction is not trusted as a single source of truth.

src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts, useCompletedTimestamp.ts, and useProcessingTimestamp.ts each wrap useLiveRelativeTimestamp with similar adapter logic, increasing wrapper count for small behavior differences.
src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts exposes class API, lazy getter, and a forwarded facade object with one-line method pass-throughs for every method.
src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts imports the forwarded facade (dataFreshnessManager), adding an extra hop with no semantic change versus direct instance usage.

src/shared/lib/ui/cn.ts is a pure re-export wrapper; high-import UI modules (e.g., src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx, src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx) add an extra alias layer without behavior.
src/shared/lib/tasks/orchestrationContract.ts returns Record<string, unknown>, so producers and consumers are not compile-time aligned on contract shape.
src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts emits orchestration fields in multiple locations/keys (e.g., orchestration_contract plus other top-level orchestration keys).

supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts must query multiple legacy param paths (orchestrator_run_id, run_id, orchestrator_details.run_id and similar for task IDs), indicating no single honest interface.
src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts catches errors, calls handleError(...), then rethrows; failure propagates after normalization.
src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts calls handleError for OAuth failures but does not rethrow/return structured failure, so callers cannot uniformly react.

src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts throws raw Error objects from Supabase branches while onError handlers also call handleError, creating mixed throw-vs-handle semantics across similar boundaries.
supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts uses JSON error responses and logger returns, while shared client modules use thrown exceptions, yielding inconsistent cross-layer failure contracts.

findings.summary: Global error handler is a high-fan-in hub that mixes core error policy with browser notification transport.
evidence: `handleError.ts` owns normalization/logging (`normalizeAppError`, `logAppError`) and UI-facing event dispatch/listener registration (`CustomEvent`, `configureErrorNotifier`) with module-level mutable singletons.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` imports `handleError` for feature-local flows, so many domain handlers become transitively coupled to shared global notifier behavior and lifecycle constraints.
findings.suggestion: Split `handleError` into a pure core error service (normalize/log/return) and a separate app-shell adapter for notification transport; have feature modules depend only on the core contract and wire notifier transport at composition root.

findings.summary: Shared tooltip module conflates primitive UI wrapper ownership with app-specific touch interaction behavior.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` exports both primitive wrappers and `TouchableTooltip`, including behaviorful state and `document.addEventListener('pointerdown', ...)` logic.
Feature modules (`CreativePartnerPane.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`) import from the same shared UI module, so design-system changes and touch-policy changes share one blast radius.
findings.suggestion: Separate primitive tooltip exports from higher-level interaction patterns (for example `ui/tooltip-primitives` vs `patterns/touchable-tooltip`) so feature-specific behavior evolves independently from core UI contracts.

findings.summary: Media gallery coordination relies on large context/prop bags across modules, making ownership boundaries difficult to reason about.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryController.ts` defines deeply nested `shotContext`, `taskDetailsContext`, and `mobileContext` bags and mainly remaps them into other hooks.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` defines additional grouped interfaces and mixes shot navigation, task modal control, and selection synchronization.
`ShotActions.tsx` depends on multiple grouped prop contracts (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`), indicating orchestration spread rather than clear vertical ownership.
findings.suggestion: Create a single feature-level gallery controller boundary (state + intents) and pass smaller intent-focused props to leaf components; keep shot-selection, task-details, and mobile gesture concerns in separate sub-controllers with explicit interfaces.

findings.summary: Install instruction scenario logic and visual key registry are manually synchronized across files, increasing coupling and drift risk.
evidence: `scenarios.ts` returns string keys from `resolveInstallVisualKey`, while `visuals.tsx` separately defines the `InstallVisualKey` union and registry mapping.
`InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` bridges both pieces (`resolveInstallVisualKey` + `installVisualRegistry[visualKey]`), so adding a scenario requires synchronized edits across multiple files.
findings.suggestion: Move to a single declarative install-scenario table that binds predicates, title, and visual component in one source of truth, and derive the key type from the table instead of maintaining a manual union.

findings.summary: Adjacent page preloader acts as a multi-concern orchestrator that couples caching, network prefetch, and image preload lifecycle in one hook.
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` performs query key inspection/removal, query prefetch, preload queue management, and loaded-image cleanup in one module.
Its own file header documents consolidation of previously separate behaviors, indicating one growing architectural hotspot for gallery runtime behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split preloader responsibilities into composable services (query prefetch policy, image preload policy, cache-retention policy) and keep the hook as a thin coordinator over those explicit collaborators.

findings.summary: Task orchestration data crosses module boundaries in multiple incompatible shapes, forcing fallback-heavy readers and query conditions.
evidence: `payloadBuilder` emits mixed top-level/orchestrator_details-style fields and optional arrays (e.g., `run_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, `*_expanded`, `phase_config`) in one payload object (lines 119-216).

`VideoTravelDetails` reconstructs meaning by checking 3-4 parallel sources for each field (`individual_segment_params`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `orchestrator_details`, top-level), including prompts, phase config, negative prompts, and model/resolution (lines 95-233).
`findSiblingSegments` and final-step lookup in complete_task use OR queries over multiple JSON paths for the same keys (lines 191-209, 239), indicating no single canonical handoff shape.
`createGenerationFromTask` explicitly warns that `full_orchestrator_payload` is passthrough and should not drive routing (lines 69-74), confirming semantic ambiguity at the seam.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce one canonical orchestration envelope (versioned if needed) with a shared parser/serializer used by task creators, completion functions, and details UI; migrate readers to canonical-only lookups, then remove fallback paths and OR-query key variants behind a compatibility window.

findings.summary: Inline edit state is repeatedly reshaped across layers, creating high local indirection and brittle internals.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` builds a large grouped model (`mode/canvas/reposition/panel/form/status`) in `buildImageEditValueModels` (lines 260-349), then separately rebuilds another large grouped return object (`canvasEnvironment/inpaintingState/transformState/generationState`) (lines 457-524).
`useImageEditValue` performs another broad mapping pass from grouped models into `ImageEditState` via multiple partial builders (lines 160-260), adding another transformation hop.

`InlineEditView` consumes deeply nested state paths (`state.inpaintingState.*`, `state.generationState.*`, `state.canvasEnvironment.*`) across multiple subcomponents, amplifying coupling to this reshaped structure.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to a single canonical edit-state model exposed by one hook, and have UI components consume that model directly (or narrow selectors); remove duplicated reshaping layers and delete legacy shape scaffolding once callers migrate.

findings.summary: Button group prop assembly introduces repetitive glue layers without meaningful boundary simplification.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` has four near-identical builders that repeatedly copy the same shared fields plus pass-through handler fields (lines 56-131).
The hook memoizes on `[params]` (line 142), which defeats stable memoization unless caller object identity is externally stabilized.

`LightboxLayout` immediately spreads these bags into concrete controls (`BottomLeftControls`, `BottomRightControls`, `TopRightControls`, lines 226-228), so the wrapper layer mostly forwards data instead of isolating policy.
findings.suggestion: Replace per-group builders with a single typed control-model factory (or inline construction at the composition site) and use explicit dependency arrays per primitive input; keep only logic-bearing transforms in the seam.

findings.summary: Shot editor callback internals duplicate control flow through layered result and adapter wrappers, increasing local complexity.
evidence: Each action is implemented as `handle*Result` with custom error object + `runCallback`, then wrapped again by `handle*Adapter` to convert return shapes (lines 177-247).
This pattern repeats for add-to-shot, add-without-position, and create-shot, multiplying function count and branch paths for equivalent operations.
The same module already has a dedicated deletion path (`useDeleteSegmentHandler`) with separate behavior (lines 175 onward), making callback style inconsistent within one file.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one callback contract for this hook (either throw-on-failure or typed result, not both), remove adapter duplication, and centralize error instrumentation in one helper that returns a single stable action shape.

findings.summary: Edge integration boundaries mix throw-based and result-object failures, making caller handling unpredictable.
evidence: `_shared/edgeOperation.ts` defines canonical `EdgeOperationResult` (`ok`, `policy`, `errorCode/message`).
`_shared/billing.ts` returns `EdgeOperationResult<void>` from `triggerCostCalculation` for both success and failure paths.
`complete_task/storage.ts` uses `EdgeOperationResult` for `thumbnailOutcome` but throws `new Error(...)` on upload failure in `handleStorageOperations`.
`_shared/rateLimit.ts` introduces a separate `RateLimitResult` contract with different fields (`allowed`, `remaining`, `policy`), bypassing shared edge result semantics.
findings.suggestion: Standardize edge function internals on one boundary contract (prefer `EdgeOperationResult`), convert thrown infrastructure errors into typed failures at helper boundaries, and only translate to HTTP responses at the top-level handler.

findings.summary: Generation invalidation has competing scheduling APIs with different lifecycle guarantees.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` keeps per-shot timeout state and cancels/replaces existing delayed invalidations (`timeoutRefs`).
`scheduleGenerationsInvalidation` schedules independent timeouts with no dedupe or cancellation, so callers can stack redundant invalidations for the same shot.
`invalidateVariantChange` adds yet another timing path (`await wait(delayMs)`) and mixes `refetchQueries` with `invalidateQueries`, creating inconsistent refresh behavior for sibling callers.
`timelineWriteQueue.ts` explicitly serializes write operations per shot, indicating this domain already requires strict sequencing, but invalidation sequencing is not aligned to that model.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one invalidation scheduler with explicit dedupe/cancel semantics keyed by shot/project and consistent refresh strategy (`invalidate` vs `refetch`), then route all call sites through it.

findings.summary: Import-time Supabase singletons in frontend integration modules make seam behavior hard to substitute and test.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.ts`, `useLineageChain.ts`, `generationTransformers.ts`, and `generateVideoService.ts` each call `getSupabaseClient()` at module scope and close over `supabase`.
These modules combine orchestration/data transformation with direct DB access, so collaborators cannot inject alternate implementations at call boundaries.
In contrast, `complete_task/storage.ts` accepts `supabase` as a parameter, showing a cleaner seam where dependencies are explicit.
findings.suggestion: Move Supabase dependency to function parameters (or factory-created services) for orchestration modules, and keep pure transformers/hooks free of hidden client acquisition.

findings.summary: Shot-selection handoff uses divergent payload contracts across event bus, context, and editor props.
evidence: `typedEvents.ts` defines `selectShotForAddition` detail as `{ shotId: string; shotName: string }`.
`ShotAdditionSelectionContext.tsx` API only carries `shotId` (`selectedShotId`, `selectShotForAddition(shotId)`), discarding name-level data from event shape.
`ShotImagesEditor/types.ts` keeps `selectedShotId` and optional `shotName` separately in `ShotImagesEditorImageState`, reinforcing parallel representations rather than one canonical selection object.
findings.suggestion: Define one `ShotSelection` boundary type and use adapters at entry points (event listener/context provider/editor props) so all seams carry the same fields and intent.

findings.summary: Prop-group resolvers flatten large cross-layer interfaces into single bags, obscuring ownership and introducing silent overwrite risk.
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` merges `displayOptions`, `imageState`, `editActions`, and `shotWorkflow` via spread order into one resolved object.
`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps` similarly merges `clipSettings`, `motionConfig`, and `uiState` into one flattened surface.
These seam helpers turn structured boundaries into broad ad-hoc contracts where additions can shadow previous keys without explicit compile-time signaling at the integration point.
findings.suggestion: Keep grouped prop objects at component seams (or use explicit namespaced accessors) and avoid flattening unless there is a strict, validated key schema.

findings.summary: Travel request construction keeps a deprecated legacy adapter path that duplicates local mapping logic.
evidence: generateVideoService defines both BuildTravelRequestBodyLegacyParams and BuildTravelRequestBodyV2Params, then maps legacy->V2 via toV2TravelRequestParams and exposes buildTravelRequestBodyFromLegacy marked @deprecated (lines ~477-622).
types.ts still exposes TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput as canonical params plus Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput> (lines ~348-354), so the legacy shape remains in normal call flow.
findings.suggestion: Remove the legacy adapter/export once callers are migrated, collapse to one request-builder signature, and keep legacy decoding only at ingestion boundaries.

findings.summary: Per-pair override assignment relies on index alignment across differently ordered collections.
evidence: buildTimelinePairConfig computes frameGaps from sortedPositions (sorted by timeline frame) but fills pairPrompts/pairPhaseConfigs/pairMotionSettings from filteredShotGenerations iteration order (lines ~295-377).
This helper is exported and reusable; if caller ordering changes, override arrays can bind to wrong pairs without explicit keying.
arrayUtils.ts already acknowledges stale-order/length scenarios by truncating arrays to target count, which reinforces that order-sensitive array logic is an active concern in this area.
findings.suggestion: Derive both gaps and per-pair overrides from one sorted pair list keyed by start/end frame or pair shot_generation IDs, then map outputs from that single source.

findings.summary: Aspect-ratio utility logic is duplicated in two helpers with partially different behavior, increasing drift risk.
evidence: imageAspectRatio.ts resolves dimensions from metadata/originalParams/project ratio and returns stringified numeric aspect ratio (lines ~35-69).
image-utils.ts implements a separate getAspectRatioStyle with only project-ratio fallback and same default of '1' (lines ~55-66).
Both functions return the same shape but with different source precedence and parsing scope.
findings.suggestion: Centralize to one shared aspect-ratio resolver and let call sites pass optional metadata/context inputs rather than maintaining separate implementations.

findings.summary: Travel task classification uses substring matching, which is imprecise relative to exact-key config flows.
evidence: isTravelTaskType returns TRAVEL_TYPES.has(taskType) || taskType.includes('travel') (taskTypeUtils.ts lines ~44-48).
taskConfig.ts resolves visibility/category/display using exact task-type keys from cache/fallback registries (lines ~47-109), so fuzzy positives can diverge from configured semantics.
findings.suggestion: Replace substring matching with an explicit registry (or prefix policy with strict delimiter checks) shared with task-type config sources.

findings.summary: Thumbnail generation keeps dead parameters and redundant path recomputation, indicating migration residue in internals.
evidence: generateThumbnail signature includes unused _sourceBytes, _userId, _taskId parameters and optional objectPath (storage.ts lines ~163-170).

handleThumbnail recomputes objectPath and passes it to generateThumbnail even though taskOutput path generation is already centralized and used earlier in upload flow (storage.ts lines ~65-67 and ~145-149).
This leaves function internals carrying stale arguments and duplicate path responsibilities.
findings.suggestion: Simplify generateThumbnail to accept only required inputs (supabase, mainImageUrl, objectPath), remove dead args, and pass the previously computed objectPath through the flow once.

findings.summary: Domain-specific generation UI is placed in `shared/components`, obscuring ownership boundaries.
evidence: `ActiveLoRAsDisplay` contains LoRA-specific matching and trigger-word behavior tied to generation semantics (`Model ID`, `trigger_word`, preview video inference).
`AspectRatioSelector` is coupled to generation aspect-ratio catalog (`ASPECT_RATIO_TO_RESOLUTION`) and local visualizer behavior, not a broadly reusable primitive.
findings.suggestion: Move these components to a generation/tool-scoped package (for example `src/domains/generation/components` or tool-specific directories), keep only UI primitives in `shared/components`, and expose thin domain exports from one barrel per domain.

findings.summary: `shared/hooks` contains feature orchestration that crosses data-access, backend invocation, and cache policy layers.
evidence: `useAutoTopup` performs auth/session checks, database reads on `users`, edge-function calls (`setup-auto-topup`), and cache invalidation from one hook.
`useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator` owns queueing, thumbnail generation service invocation, error reporting, and direct query cache mutation/invalidation.
findings.suggestion: Split hooks into layered modules: domain service (backend/data orchestration), cache adapter/query key policy, and UI-facing hook composition. Place feature-specific hooks under a domain/feature folder rather than global `shared/hooks`.

findings.summary: Repository-level config and colocated hook tests show unresolved structural drift from earlier package layouts.
evidence: `tailwind.config.ts` includes root globs (`./pages`, `./components`, `./app`) that do not match the current `src/*` organization.
`vite.config.ts` stores environment host allowlists and detailed chunk-vendor policy inline, coupling deployment/runtime policy into the main bundler config file.

Tests mock old paths (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`) while implementations import reorganized modules (`errorHandling/handleError`, `queryKeys/credits`), signaling package contract drift.
findings.suggestion: Realign config to current package map (remove dead root globs, extract deployment/chunk policy into clearly named config modules), and update test import/mocking contracts to authoritative module entry points to prevent further boundary erosion.

findings.summary: Documented startup contract for error notifications is not enforced in runtime behavior.
evidence: README.md states notifier wiring should be registered once at app startup via configureErrorNotifier(...).
handleError() always dispatches a window event when toasting, but there is no guard, warning, or fail-fast path when no notifier listener has been registered.
findings.suggestion: Add a contract check in handleError/configureErrorNotifier (at least in development) that warns or throws when toast emission is attempted before notifier registration, and document the fallback semantics explicitly.

findings.summary: Generation domain type surface is routed through global type modules, blurring ownership boundaries.
evidence: src/domains/generation/types/index.ts re-exports only from '@/types/generationParams', '@/types/generationMetadata', '@/types/generationRow', and '@/types/shot'.
This creates a domain entrypoint that depends on non-domain source locations for its core contracts, reducing clarity of where generation contracts are owned.
findings.suggestion: Make domain types canonical in the domain folder (or explicitly mark this index as a temporary compatibility facade with deprecation policy) so ownership and change boundaries are predictable.

findings.summary: A high-use UI utility alias introduces architectural indirection without explicit contract guidance.
evidence: src/shared/lib/ui/cn.ts is a single-line re-export of cn from '@/shared/lib/uiClassNames'.
Core UI primitives like src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx import cn through the alias path, making the alias part of the practical public surface.
README runtime contracts enumerate several shared runtime ownership rules but do not define policy for compatibility alias modules like this.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical import path for class-name composition (or formally document alias policy and lifecycle), then align imports across shared UI modules to reduce undocumented surface indirection.

findings.summary: Orchestration metadata is effectively untyped and consumed through multi-path fallbacks, so producers and consumers disagree on the true contract.
evidence: buildOrchestrationContract returns Record<string, unknown> instead of a concrete type.
Payload builders emit orchestration-related fields both inside and outside orchestration_contract.
Orchestrator completion queries several alternate JSON paths for run/task identifiers, which is a runtime compatibility workaround for schema drift.
findings.suggestion: Define a strict shared OrchestrationContract type/schema, enforce a single canonical storage path, and add a single compatibility parser at boundary entrypoints to translate legacy payloads once.

findings.summary: Data freshness access is wrapped by parallel APIs (class, getter, forwarded facade), adding call indirection without additional policy value.
evidence: DataFreshnessManager.ts defines getDataFreshnessManager plus a dataFreshnessManager object that forwards every method to getOrCreateDataFreshnessManager.
useTasks.ts uses the facade methods directly, so behavior depends on forwarding wrappers rather than a single explicit API.
findings.suggestion: Choose one public API surface (prefer explicit getter + concrete instance usage), deprecate the forwarded facade, and migrate consumers to reduce mental and debugging hops.

findings.summary: Device detection is split across overlapping hooks and local fallbacks, so the abstraction does not provide a trusted single source of truth.
evidence: useMobile.tsx computes similar signals in several hooks with separate listeners and state lifecycles.
MediaGallery consumes useIsMobile/useIsTablet and still adds a manual window-width fallback, indicating abstraction leakage and low caller confidence.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to one canonical device-state hook/object, expose derived flags from that source, and remove per-consumer fallbacks unless explicitly justified.

findings.summary: Error handling alternates between throw-only, handle-and-rethrow, and handle-without-propagation, making failure behavior unpredictable across similar operations.
evidence: magicEdit task creation catches, normalizes via handleError, then throws.
useDiscordSignIn normalizes errors but does not rethrow or return a structured failure result.
useGenerationMutations throws raw Error in data-layer helpers while mutation onError also routes through handleError.
Edge function handlers return HTTP JSON errors instead of exception propagation, diverging from client-side throw-based contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define per-layer error contracts (e.g., data layer throws typed errors, UI layer converts to user notifications, edge layer returns standardized error payload schema) and apply consistently across hooks/task builders.

findings.summary: Label exposes variant typing but has no actual variant definitions, creating an interface that promises flexibility it does not implement.
evidence: label.tsx uses VariantProps<typeof labelVariants> even though labelVariants has no variants configured.
button.tsx demonstrates a real variant/size API, highlighting mismatch in component interface semantics across adjacent UI primitives.
findings.suggestion: Either remove VariantProps from Label to make the API honest, or add explicit label variants and corresponding class mappings.
